generated_description: Adversaries may directly access the memory of the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) process to extract credentials. This behavior involves an unexpected process, such as VBoxService.exe, obtaining specific access rights to the LSASS memory space. By directly interacting with LSASS memory, adversaries can harvest authentication material, enabling lateral movement or persistent access within an environment.

generated_description: An adversary may leverage legitimate system binaries to invoke Component Object Model (COM) objects for execution. In this instance, a system process spawned the Windows Update Client, which then explicitly invoked a COM server identified by a specific Class ID using a deployment provider DLL and the `/RunHandlerComServer` flag. This action led to the execution of additional binaries, including an anti-malware delta update executable and a signature stub, which created and subsequently deleted temporary files during their operation.

Adversaries may abuse Regsvr32.exe to proxy execution of malicious code. Regsvr32.exe is a command-line program used to register and unregister object linking and embedding controls, including dynamic link libraries (DLLs), on Windows systems. The Regsvr32.exe binary may also be signed by Microsoft.
